lts.lt_definition: I can identify the legal rights and protections associated with different family structures including marriage, civil partnership, and cohabitation.
I can explain how families change over time and describe the characteristics of effective parenting and child development.
I can explain the principles of consent, trust, respect, and ethical behaviour that characterise healthy romantic relationships.
I can judge when a relationship is unsafe and identify where to seek help when needed.
I can explain the importance of equality, self-esteem, and respectful treatment in relationships.
I can communicate respectfully in relationships and conflicts with others and strangers.
The student treats others with kindness and respect while developing personal interests and skills.
I can identify different types of bullying and explain their impact on victims.
I can determine appropriate sources and methods for getting help regarding bullying situations.
The student holds responsibility for reporting bullying when witnessed as a bystander.
I can end relationships or friendships using respectful communication that acknowledges the other person's feelings.
I can identify and cope with difficult emotions like disappointment, hurt, or frustration when relationships end.
I can explain how stereotypes based on protected characteristics can cause damage and encourage prejudice.
I can identify misogyny and other forms of prejudice when they occur.
I can analyse how power inequalities affect behaviour and respect within relationships.
I can explain how pornography negatively influences sexual attitudes and behaviours through normalisation of harm and power imbalances.
I can discuss how subcultures influence understanding of sexual ethics and analyse their impact on sexual norms.
I can identify appropriate privacy settings and explain why personal information should be protected in different online contexts.
I can identify potential signs of fake accounts, AI-generated content, and exaggerated profiles on social media platforms.
I can explain how behavioural expectations remain the same across online and offline contexts.

lts.lt_name: Understanding Digital Content Risks and Laws
lts.lt_definition: I can explain the risks and legal consequences of sharing images online, including criminal penalties for indecent images.

lts.lt_definition: The student practises restraint in sharing personal material and refuses to forward inappropriate content received from others.

lts.lt_definition: I can identify deepfakes and inappropriate online content, understanding their prevalence and potential harms.

lts.competency_name: Identifying Deepfakes and Harmful Online Content
lts.lt_definition: I can explain where to seek advice and support after exposure to harmful online content.

lt_names: Understanding Support Resources and Content Impact

lts.lt_definition: I can identify when technology is being used for bullying, harassment, stalking, or coercive behaviour.
I can explain how pornography and other online content presents distorted pictures that negatively affect behaviour and attitudes.
I can identify appropriate sources of support when encountering concerning online behaviour or content.

lts.competency_name: Online Scams and Emerging Technology Risks
lts.lt_definition: I can identify online scams, sextortion risks, and threats posed by emerging technologies including AI chatbots.

lts.lt_name: Evaluating and Responding to Digital Risks
lts.lt_definition: I can critically evaluate new technologies for potential risks and seek appropriate support when encountering scams or sextortion.

lts.lt_name: Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries
lts.lt_definition: I can recognise, communicate, and respect consent and boundaries in romantic and sexual relationships across all contexts.

lt_names: Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries
Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries

lts.lt_definition: I can identify and resist pressure in relationships while avoiding putting pressure on others.

lt_names: Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries
Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries

lts.lt_definition: I can explain how kindness and care for others requires more than just consent.

lt_names: Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries
Recognising and Communicating Consent and Boundaries

lts.lt_definition: I can assess whether people and situations are trustworthy and safe using appropriate criteria and reasoning.
I can identify appropriate sources of help and use effective strategies to seek assistance or report concerns.
The student trusts their instincts and acts on feelings when situations don't feel right.
I can identify what constitutes sexual harassment and violence including specific behaviours and concerning actions.
I can explain that sexual harassment and violence are never the fault of the victim.
I can identify the key concepts and legal definitions of domestic abuse, exploitation, and related harmful practices.
I can explain the legal framework and statutory protections provided by domestic abuse legislation.

lts.competency_name: Recognising Harmful Sexual Norms and Seeking Support
lts.lt_name: Recognising Harmful Sexual Norms in Pornography
lts.lt_definition: I can identify how pornography normalises activities that can be emotionally or physically harmful.

Identifying Harmful Sexual Norms in Pornography
Identifying Harmful Sexual Norms in Media

lts.competency_name: Recognising Harmful Sexual Norms and Seeking Support
lts.lt_definition: I can explain how to seek support for worrying or abusive sexual behaviour.

lts.lt_definition: I can explain the principles of sexual consent and the conditions that make sexual activity positive and appropriate.
I can analyse how kindness, care, and health considerations extend beyond consent in sexual relationships.
I can identify the full range of contraceptive choices and their efficacy rates.
I can locate medically accurate online information to support contraceptive and pregnancy decision-making.
I can describe methods to reduce sexual health risks including safer sex practices and prevention medications.
I can identify where to access confidential sexual health services and support for relationship concerns.

lts.competency_name: Emotional Wellbeing and Mental Health Literacy
lts.lt_definition: I can talk about my emotions accurately and sensitively using appropriate vocabulary.

lts.competency_name: Emotional Wellbeing and Mental Health Literacy
lts.lt_definition: I can distinguish between normal feelings and mental health conditions and identify factors that affect wellbeing.
I can identify the mental health harms associated with gambling and explain how different gambling products pose varying levels of risk.
I can explain the bidirectional relationships between alcohol, drug use, smoking and mental health outcomes.
I can explain how online behaviour and content affect mental and physical wellbeing for myself and others.
I can critically assess the reliability and intent of online information and targeted content.
I can identify appropriate reporting and support mechanisms when encountering harmful online content or behaviours.
I can identify the links between poor dietary choices and specific health risks including tooth decay, weight gain, and cardiovascular disease.
I can identify the health risks associated with unhealthy weight gain including cancer, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.
I can identify the health risks, legal consequences, and safety concerns associated with illegal drugs, alcohol, and tobacco products.

lt_names: Understanding Illegal Drug Risks and Legal Status
Understanding Alcohol Consumption Risks and Safety
Understanding Tobacco and Vaping Health Impacts

lts.lt_definition: I can describe strategies to increase personal safety when around alcohol and other substances.

lt_names: Understanding Illegal Drug Risks and Legal Status
Understanding Alcohol Consumption Risks and Safety
Understanding Tobacco and Vaping Health Impacts

lts.lt_definition: I can explain infection prevention methods, antibiotic use, vaccination benefits, and the importance of regular self-examination.
I can describe dental hygiene practices, sleep hygiene requirements, and reproductive health considerations including pre-conception care.
I can identify which healthcare services to access for different health needs and situations.
I can explain the legal frameworks governing medical consent for minors and adults with capacity issues.

lts.lt_definition: I can identify potential safety hazards in various environments and apply appropriate risk management strategies.

Identifying and Managing Environmental Safety Risks
Identifying and Managing Environmental Safety Risks

lts.lt_definition: I can recognise when peer influence is affecting my safety decisions and apply strategies to resist harmful pressure.

lts.lt_definition: I can explain the actual prevalence of weapon carrying among young people and identify misconceptions that lead to false beliefs about needing weapons for protection.
I can identify appropriate sources of help and take action when there are concerns about grooming or exploitation.
I can identify and describe common menstrual problems, reproductive health facts, and fertility factors.
